game_identity.visual_style: Cartoonish, 2D vector art with physics-based destruction

core_loop.one_sentence: Players aim and fire various projectiles from their mobile castle to destroy the opponent's castle before their own is destroyed.
core_loop.player_goal: Reduce the enemy castle's health to 0% by hitting it with projectiles.
core_loop.failure_or_pressure: The enemy castle fires back, reducing the player's castle health.
core_loop.why_it_is_fun: Satisfying physics destruction, tactical aiming, and discovering different projectile types.

player_controls.result: Displays a trajectory line while dragging, and fires a projectile upon release.

visible_ui.purpose: Indicates the remaining health of each castle.
Emphasizes the competitive nature of the game.
Call to action to download or play the game.

timeline_beats.gameplay_meaning: Establishes the threat and the turn-based or continuous attack nature of the game.
timeline_beats.playable_relevance: Good hook to show the player they are under attack and need to respond.

timeline_beats.event: Player aims and fires a single rocket
timeline_beats.gameplay_meaning: Introduces the core aiming mechanic and basic attack.
timeline_beats.playable_relevance: The primary interaction the user will perform.

timeline_beats.event: Player uses a different character to fire multiple rockets
timeline_beats.gameplay_meaning: Shows variety in attacks and characters.
timeline_beats.playable_relevance: Demonstrates depth and strategy; different units have different abilities.

timeline_beats.event: Player uses a goblin to fire an explosive bomb
timeline_beats.gameplay_meaning: Further showcases unit variety and different destruction effects.
timeline_beats.playable_relevance: Keeps the gameplay loop interesting with varied visual feedback.

mechanics.name: Slingshot Aiming
mechanics.description: Dragging from a character creates a dotted trajectory line indicating where the projectile will go.

mechanics.name: Physics Destruction
mechanics.description: Castles are composed of blocks/sections that break apart, fall, and disappear when hit by projectiles.

mechanics.name: Unit Types
mechanics.description: Different characters fire different projectiles (e.g., single rocket, multi-rocket, bomb).

mechanics.name: Character Death
mechanics.description: When a section of the castle containing a character is destroyed, the character turns into a tombstone.

feedback_and_juice.visual_or_audio_feedback: Explosions, smoke, flying debris, screen shake, and floating damage numbers (e.g., -140, -44).
Dotted trajectory line updates in real-time based on drag position.
Health bar decreases and percentage text updates dynamically.

must_include: At least one type of projectile to fire
can_simplify: Complex physics destruction can be simplified to pre-fractured chunks or simple health-based state changes for castle sections
Reduce the number of playable characters to 1 or 2 for a shorter ad experience
should_avoid: Complex enemy AI; enemy can just fire on a simple timer
Moving the castles (tracks are visible but castles remain stationary in the video)

variation_hooks.effect: Changes the visual effect and damage pattern (e.g., single heavy hit vs. spread damage).
Alters the optimal aiming strategy for the player.

open_questions_or_assumptions: Are the castles supposed to move? They have tank treads, but remain stationary throughout the clip.
Is the enemy's targeting random, or does it aim at specific weak points/characters?
Does the player choose which character to use, or is it a set sequence? The video shows the player selecting different characters.
